---
title: "Advanced Lighting"
subtitle: "Computer Graphics - Multiple Lights and Light Types"
author: "Gustavo Reis"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dracula
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    logo: "images/OpenGL/Opengl-logo.svg"
    css: custom.css
    footer: "Advanced Lighting Techniques"
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    highlight-style: github-dark
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
---

## From Basic to Advanced

We've covered the **Phong lighting model** with a single light source.

::: {.fragment}
**Real scenes** have multiple lights of different types:

- Point lights (light bulbs)
- Directional lights (sun)
- Spotlights (flashlights)
- Area lights
:::

::: {.fragment}
Today we'll implement a **flexible lighting system** that handles multiple lights efficiently!
:::

---

# Light Types

## Directional Lights

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](https://learnopengl.com/img/lighting/light_casters_directional.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Properties:**

- Infinitely far away
- All rays parallel
- Same direction everywhere
- No attenuation

**Examples:**

- Sun
- Moon
- Distant light sources
:::
:::

## Directional Light in GLSL

```glsl
struct DirLight {
    vec3 direction;  // Direction TO the light
    
    vec3 ambient;
    vec3 diffuse;
    vec3 specular;
};

uniform DirLight dirLight;

vec3 CalcDirLight(DirLight light, vec3 normal, vec3 viewDir) {
    vec3 lightDir = normalize(-light.direction);
    
    // Diffuse
    float diff = max(dot(normal, lightDir), 0.0);
    
    // Specular
    vec3 reflectDir = reflect(-lightDir, normal);
    float spec = pow(max(dot(viewDir, reflectDir), 0.0), material.shininess);
    
    // Combine
    vec3 ambient = light.ambient * texture(material.diffuse, TexCoords).rgb;
    vec3 diffuse = light.diffuse * diff * texture(material.diffuse, TexCoords).rgb;
    vec3 specular = light.specular * spec * texture(material.specular, TexCoords).rgb;
    
    return ambient + diffuse + specular;
}
```

## Point Lights

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](https://learnopengl.com/img/lighting/light_casters_point.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Properties:**

- Specific position in space
- Radiates in all directions
- **Attenuation** based on distance
- Intensity decreases with distance

**Examples:**

- Light bulbs
- Torches
- Candles
:::
:::

## Light Attenuation

::: {.callout-important}
Light intensity decreases with distance according to physics!
:::

$$F_{att} = \frac{1.0}{K_c + K_l \cdot d + K_q \cdot d^2}$$

::: {.incremental}
- $K_c$: **Constant** term (usually 1.0)
- $K_l$: **Linear** term
- $K_q$: **Quadratic** term
- $d$: Distance to light
:::

## Attenuation Values Table

| Distance | Constant | Linear | Quadratic |
|----------|----------|--------|-----------|
| 7        | 1.0      | 0.7    | 1.8       |
| 13       | 1.0      | 0.35   | 0.44      |
| 20       | 1.0      | 0.22   | 0.20      |
| 32       | 1.0      | 0.14   | 0.07      |
| 50       | 1.0      | 0.09   | 0.032     |
| 65       | 1.0      | 0.07   | 0.017     |
| 100      | 1.0      | 0.045  | 0.0075    |
| 160      | 1.0      | 0.027  | 0.0028    |
| 200      | 1.0      | 0.022  | 0.0019    |
| 325      | 1.0      | 0.014  | 0.0007    |
| 600      | 1.0      | 0.007  | 0.0002    |

## Point Light Structure

```glsl
struct PointLight {
    vec3 position;
    
    float constant;
    float linear;
    float quadratic;
    
    vec3 ambient;
    vec3 diffuse;
    vec3 specular;
};

uniform PointLight pointLights[NR_POINT_LIGHTS];
```

## Point Light Calculation {.smaller}

```glsl
vec3 CalcPointLight(PointLight light, vec3 normal, vec3 fragPos, vec3 viewDir) {
    vec3 lightDir = normalize(light.position - fragPos);
    
    // Diffuse shading
    float diff = max(dot(normal, lightDir), 0.0);
    
    // Specular shading
    vec3 reflectDir = reflect(-lightDir, normal);
    float spec = pow(max(dot(viewDir, reflectDir), 0.0), material.shininess);
    
    // Attenuation
    float distance = length(light.position - fragPos);
    float attenuation = 1.0 / (light.constant + light.linear * distance +
                               light.quadratic * (distance * distance));
    
    // Combine results
    vec3 ambient = light.ambient * texture(material.diffuse, TexCoords).rgb;
    vec3 diffuse = light.diffuse * diff * texture(material.diffuse, TexCoords).rgb;
    vec3 specular = light.specular * spec * texture(material.specular, TexCoords).rgb;
    
    ambient *= attenuation;
    diffuse *= attenuation;
    specular *= attenuation;
    
    return ambient + diffuse + specular;
}
```

## Spotlights

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](https://learnopengl.com/img/lighting/light_casters_spotlight.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Properties:**

- Positioned at specific location
- Points in specific direction
- Light constrained to **cone**
- Has **cutoff angle**
- **Soft edges** possible

**Examples:**

- Flashlight
- Street lamps
- Stage spotlights
:::
:::

## Spotlight Cutoff

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
![](https://learnopengl.com/img/lighting/light_casters_spotlight_angles.png)
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
**Cutoff angles:**

- $\phi$: Inner cutoff (full intensity)
- $\gamma$: Outer cutoff (zero intensity)
- Smooth **falloff** between them

**Calculation:**

- Use dot product
- Compare with cosine of angles
:::
:::

## Spotlight Structure

```glsl
struct SpotLight {
    vec3 position;
    vec3 direction;
    float cutOff;        // cos(inner angle)
    float outerCutOff;   // cos(outer angle)
    
    float constant;
    float linear;
    float quadratic;
    
    vec3 ambient;
    vec3 diffuse;
    vec3 specular;
};

uniform SpotLight spotLight;
```

## Spotlight Calculation {.smaller}

```glsl
vec3 CalcSpotLight(SpotLight light, vec3 normal, vec3 fragPos, vec3 viewDir) {
    vec3 lightDir = normalize(light.position - fragPos);
    
    // Diffuse shading
    float diff = max(dot(normal, lightDir), 0.0);
    
    // Specular shading
    vec3 reflectDir = reflect(-lightDir, normal);
    float spec = pow(max(dot(viewDir, reflectDir), 0.0), material.shininess);
    
    // Attenuation
    float distance = length(light.position - fragPos);
    float attenuation = 1.0 / (light.constant + light.linear * distance +
                               light.quadratic * (distance * distance));
    
    // Spotlight intensity (soft edges)
    float theta = dot(lightDir, normalize(-light.direction));
    float epsilon = light.cutOff - light.outerCutOff;
    float intensity = clamp((theta - light.outerCutOff) / epsilon, 0.0, 1.0);
    
    // Combine results
    vec3 ambient = light.ambient * texture(material.diffuse, TexCoords).rgb;
    vec3 diffuse = light.diffuse * diff * texture(material.diffuse, TexCoords).rgb;
    vec3 specular = light.specular * spec * texture(material.specular, TexCoords).rgb;
    
    ambient *= attenuation * intensity;
    diffuse *= attenuation * intensity;
    specular *= attenuation * intensity;
    
    return ambient + diffuse + specular;
}
```

## Smooth Edges Explanation

```glsl
// Without smooth edges (hard cutoff)
if (theta > light.cutOff) {
    // inside spotlight cone
} else {
    // outside - no light
}

// With smooth edges (better!)
float epsilon = light.cutOff - light.outerCutOff;
float intensity = clamp((theta - light.outerCutOff) / epsilon, 0.0, 1.0);
// intensity smoothly transitions from 1.0 to 0.0
```

![](https://learnopengl.com/img/lighting/light_casters_spotlight_graph.png){width=60%}

---

# Multiple Lights System

## Complete Fragment Shader {.smaller}

```glsl
#version 330 core
out vec4 FragColor;

struct Material {
    sampler2D diffuse;
    sampler2D specular;
    float shininess;
};

struct DirLight {
    vec3 direction;
    vec3 ambient, diffuse, specular;
};

struct PointLight {
    vec3 position;
    float constant, linear, quadratic;
    vec3 ambient, diffuse, specular;
};

struct SpotLight {
    vec3 position, direction;
    float cutOff, outerCutOff;
    float constant, linear, quadratic;
    vec3 ambient, diffuse, specular;
};

#define NR_POINT_LIGHTS 4

in vec3 FragPos;
in vec3 Normal;
in vec2 TexCoords;

uniform vec3 viewPos;
uniform DirLight dirLight;
uniform PointLight pointLights[NR_POINT_LIGHTS];
uniform SpotLight spotLight;
uniform Material material;

// Function prototypes
vec3 CalcDirLight(DirLight light, vec3 normal, vec3 viewDir);
vec3 CalcPointLight(PointLight light, vec3 normal, vec3 fragPos, vec3 viewDir);
vec3 CalcSpotLight(SpotLight light, vec3 normal, vec3 fragPos, vec3 viewDir);

void main() {
    vec3 norm = normalize(Normal);
    vec3 viewDir = normalize(viewPos - FragPos);
    
    // Phase 1: Directional lighting
    vec3 result = CalcDirLight(dirLight, norm, viewDir);
    
    // Phase 2: Point lights
    for(int i = 0; i < NR_POINT_LIGHTS; i++)
        result += CalcPointLight(pointLights[i], norm, FragPos, viewDir);
    
    // Phase 3: Spot light
    result += CalcSpotLight(spotLight, norm, FragPos, viewDir);
    
    FragColor = vec4(result, 1.0);
}
```

## Setting Lights from C++ {.smaller}

```cpp
shader.use();

// Directional light
shader.setVec3("dirLight.direction", glm::vec3(-0.2f, -1.0f, -0.3f));
shader.setVec3("dirLight.ambient", glm::vec3(0.05f));
shader.setVec3("dirLight.diffuse", glm::vec3(0.4f));
shader.setVec3("dirLight.specular", glm::vec3(0.5f));

// Point lights
glm::vec3 pointLightPositions[] = {
    glm::vec3( 0.7f,  0.2f,  2.0f),
    glm::vec3( 2.3f, -3.3f, -4.0f),
    glm::vec3(-4.0f,  2.0f, -12.0f),
    glm::vec3( 0.0f,  0.0f, -3.0f)
};

for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
    std::string number = std::to_string(i);
    shader.setVec3("pointLights[" + number + "].position", pointLightPositions[i]);
    shader.setVec3("pointLights[" + number + "].ambient", glm::vec3(0.05f));
    shader.setVec3("pointLights[" + number + "].diffuse", glm::vec3(0.8f));
    shader.setVec3("pointLights[" + number + "].specular", glm::vec3(1.0f));
    shader.setFloat("pointLights[" + number + "].constant", 1.0f);
    shader.setFloat("pointLights[" + number + "].linear", 0.09f);
    shader.setFloat("pointLights[" + number + "].quadratic", 0.032f);
}

// Spotlight (flashlight attached to camera)
shader.setVec3("spotLight.position", camera.Position);
shader.setVec3("spotLight.direction", camera.Front);
shader.setVec3("spotLight.ambient", glm::vec3(0.0f));
shader.setVec3("spotLight.diffuse", glm::vec3(1.0f));
shader.setVec3("spotLight.specular", glm::vec3(1.0f));
shader.setFloat("spotLight.constant", 1.0f);
shader.setFloat("spotLight.linear", 0.09f);
shader.setFloat("spotLight.quadratic", 0.032f);
shader.setFloat("spotLight.cutOff", glm::cos(glm::radians(12.5f)));
shader.setFloat("spotLight.outerCutOff", glm::cos(glm::radians(15.0f)));
```

---

# Light Visualization

## Drawing Light Sources

```cpp
// Simple light cube shader
Shader lightCubeShader("light_cube.vs", "light_cube.fs");

// For each point light
for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
    model = glm::mat4(1.0f);
    model = glm::translate(model, pointLightPositions[i]);
    model = glm::scale(model, glm::vec3(0.2f));
    
    lightCubeShader.use();
    lightCubeShader.setMat4("model", model);
    lightCubeShader.setMat4("view", view);
    lightCubeShader.setMat4("projection", projection);
    lightCubeShader.setVec3("lightColor", glm::vec3(1.0f));
    
    // Draw cube
    glBindVertexArray(lightVAO);
    glDrawArrays(GL_TRIANGLES, 0, 36);
}
```

## Light Cube Shaders

**Vertex Shader:**
```glsl
#version 330 core
layout (location = 0) in vec3 aPos;

uniform mat4 model;
uniform mat4 view;
uniform mat4 projection;

void main() {
    gl_Position = projection * view * model * vec4(aPos, 1.0);
}
```

**Fragment Shader:**
```glsl
#version 330 core
out vec4 FragColor;

uniform vec3 lightColor;

void main() {
    FragColor = vec4(lightColor, 1.0);
}
```

---

# Shadow Fundamentals

## Why Shadows Matter

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Without shadows:**
![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced-lighting/shadow_mapping_with_without.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**With shadows:**
![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced-lighting/shadow_mapping_with_without.png)

Shadows provide:

- Depth perception
- Spatial relationships
- Realism
- Visual grounding
:::
:::

## Shadow Mapping Overview

::: {.callout-important}
**Shadow Mapping**: Render scene from light's perspective, store depth, compare when rendering from camera.
:::

**Two-pass technique:**

1. **Shadow pass**: Render to depth map from light's view
2. **Rendering pass**: Compare fragment depth with shadow map

::: {.fragment}
If fragment is **further** from light than stored depth → in shadow!
:::

## Shadow Map Process

![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced-lighting/shadow_mapping_theory_spaces.png)

::: {.incremental}
1. Render scene from light POV to depth texture
2. Transform fragment position to light space
3. Compare depth with shadow map value
4. Apply shadow factor to lighting
:::

## Creating Shadow Map Framebuffer

```cpp
const unsigned int SHADOW_WIDTH = 1024, SHADOW_HEIGHT = 1024;

unsigned int depthMapFBO;
glGenFramebuffers(1, &depthMapFBO);

// Create depth texture
unsigned int depthMap;
glGenTextures(1, &depthMap);
glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, depthMap);
glTexImage2D(GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL_DEPTH_COMPONENT, 
             SHADOW_WIDTH, SHADOW_HEIGHT, 0, 
             GL_DEPTH_COMPONENT, GL_FLOAT, NULL);
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MIN_FILTER, GL_NEAREST);
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MAG_FILTER, GL_NEAREST);
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_WRAP_S, GL_CLAMP_TO_BORDER);
glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_WRAP_T, GL_CLAMP_TO_BORDER);
float borderColor[] = { 1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f };
glTexParameterfv(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_BORDER_COLOR, borderColor);

// Attach to framebuffer
glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, depthMapFBO);
glFramebufferTexture2D(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, GL_DEPTH_ATTACHMENT, 
                      GL_TEXTURE_2D, depthMap, 0);
glDrawBuffer(GL_NONE);  // No color buffer
glReadBuffer(GL_NONE);
glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, 0);
```

## Rendering Shadow Map

```cpp
// 1. Render to depth map
glViewport(0, 0, SHADOW_WIDTH, SHADOW_HEIGHT);
glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, depthMapFBO);
glClear(GL_DEPTH_BUFFER_BIT);

// Configure shader and matrices for light space
simpleDepthShader.use();
glm::mat4 lightProjection = glm::ortho(-10.0f, 10.0f, -10.0f, 10.0f, 
                                       1.0f, 7.5f);
glm::mat4 lightView = glm::lookAt(lightPos, glm::vec3(0.0f), 
                                  glm::vec3(0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f));
glm::mat4 lightSpaceMatrix = lightProjection * lightView;
simpleDepthShader.setMat4("lightSpaceMatrix", lightSpaceMatrix);

// Render scene
renderScene(simpleDepthShader);

glBindFramebuffer(GL_FRAMEBUFFER, 0);

// 2. Render scene normally with shadow mapping
glViewport(0, 0, SCR_WIDTH, SCR_HEIGHT);
glClear(GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT | GL_DEPTH_BUFFER_BIT);
shader.use();
// ... set uniforms ...
shader.setMat4("lightSpaceMatrix", lightSpaceMatrix);
glActiveTexture(GL_TEXTURE0);
glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, depthMap);
renderScene(shader);
```

## Simple Depth Shader

**Vertex Shader:**
```glsl
#version 330 core
layout (location = 0) in vec3 aPos;

uniform mat4 lightSpaceMatrix;
uniform mat4 model;

void main() {
    gl_Position = lightSpaceMatrix * model * vec4(aPos, 1.0);
}
```

**Fragment Shader:**
```glsl
#version 330 core

void main() {
    // gl_FragDepth is written automatically
}
```

## Shadow Calculation in Fragment Shader {.smaller}

```glsl
#version 330 core
out vec4 FragColor;

in VS_OUT {
    vec3 FragPos;
    vec3 Normal;
    vec2 TexCoords;
    vec4 FragPosLightSpace;
} fs_in;

uniform sampler2D diffuseTexture;
uniform sampler2D shadowMap;
uniform vec3 lightPos;
uniform vec3 viewPos;

float ShadowCalculation(vec4 fragPosLightSpace) {
    // Perspective divide
    vec3 projCoords = fragPosLightSpace.xyz / fragPosLightSpace.w;
    
    // Transform to [0,1] range
    projCoords = projCoords * 0.5 + 0.5;
    
    // Get closest depth value from light's perspective
    float closestDepth = texture(shadowMap, projCoords.xy).r;
    
    // Get depth of current fragment from light's perspective
    float currentDepth = projCoords.z;
    
    // Check if current fragment is in shadow
    float bias = 0.005;  // Prevents shadow acne
    float shadow = currentDepth - bias > closestDepth ? 1.0 : 0.0;
    
    return shadow;
}

void main() {
    vec3 color = texture(diffuseTexture, fs_in.TexCoords).rgb;
    vec3 normal = normalize(fs_in.Normal);
    vec3 lightColor = vec3(1.0);
    
    // Ambient
    vec3 ambient = 0.15 * color;
    
    // Diffuse
    vec3 lightDir = normalize(lightPos - fs_in.FragPos);
    float diff = max(dot(lightDir, normal), 0.0);
    vec3 diffuse = diff * lightColor;
    
    // Specular
    vec3 viewDir = normalize(viewPos - fs_in.FragPos);
    vec3 reflectDir = reflect(-lightDir, normal);
    float spec = pow(max(dot(viewDir, reflectDir), 0.0), 32.0);
    vec3 specular = spec * lightColor;
    
    // Calculate shadow
    float shadow = ShadowCalculation(fs_in.FragPosLightSpace);
    
    // Apply shadow (keep some ambient light)
    vec3 lighting = (ambient + (1.0 - shadow) * (diffuse + specular)) * color;
    
    FragColor = vec4(lighting, 1.0);
}
```

## Shadow Acne

![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced-lighting/shadow_mapping_acne.png)

**Problem**: Depth resolution causes striping patterns.

**Solution**: Add small **bias** to depth comparison:

```glsl
float bias = 0.005;
float shadow = currentDepth - bias > closestDepth ? 1.0 : 0.0;
```

::: {.fragment}
**Better**: Adaptive bias based on surface angle:
```glsl
float bias = max(0.05 * (1.0 - dot(normal, lightDir)), 0.005);
```
:::

## Peter Panning

![](https://learnopengl.com/img/advanced-lighting/shadow_mapping_peter_panning.png)

**Problem**: Too much bias causes objects to "float".

**Solutions:**

- Use front face culling for shadow pass
- Fine-tune bias value
- Use cascade shadow maps for large scenes

---

# Performance Optimization

## Optimization Techniques

::: {.incremental}
- **Reduce light count**: Use only necessary lights
- **Light culling**: Calculate which lights affect which objects
- **Deferred shading**: Handle many lights efficiently
- **Light baking**: Pre-calculate static lighting
- **LOD for shadows**: Lower resolution for distant shadows
- **Cascade shadow maps**: Multiple shadow maps for different distances
:::

## Light Culling Example

```cpp
struct Light {
    glm::vec3 position;
    float radius;  // Max influence distance
    // ... other properties
};

std::vector<Light> visibleLights;

for (const auto& light : allLights) {
    // Frustum culling
    if (camera.frustum.contains(light.position, light.radius)) {
        // Distance culling
        float distance = glm::length(camera.position - light.position);
        if (distance < light.radius) {
            visibleLights.push_back(light);
        }
    }
}

// Only send visible lights to shader
```

## Deferred Shading Preview

::: {.callout-tip}
**Deferred Shading**: Render geometry first, lighting second - enables hundreds of lights!
:::

**Traditional (Forward)**: Lighting per object

- Complex scenes with many lights: **expensive**
- Must calculate all lights for each fragment

**Deferred**: Lighting per light

- Render geometry data to G-buffer
- Apply lighting as screen-space pass
- **O(lights)** complexity instead of **O(objects × lights)**

---

# Best Practices

## Light Setup Guidelines

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Performance:**

- Keep light count reasonable
- Use appropriate attenuation
- Disable lights when not visible
- Consider baking static lights
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Visual Quality:**

- Mix different light types
- Use colored lights carefully
- Adjust ambient carefully
- Test in various conditions
:::
:::

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

::: {.callout-warning}
**Watch out for:**

- Too many lights (performance)
- Forgetting attenuation (unrealistic)
- Uniform ambient everywhere (flat)
- Hard spotlight edges (unrealistic)
- Wrong cutoff angle order (outer > inner)
- Missing shadow bias (acne)
- Too much bias (peter panning)
:::

---

# What We've Learned

## Key Concepts

::: {.incremental}
- **Light types**: Directional, point, spotlight
- **Attenuation**: Physical light falloff
- **Multiple lights**: Combining different sources
- **Light structures**: Efficient data organization
- **Shadow mapping**: Basic shadow technique
- **Shadow artifacts**: Acne and peter panning
- **Performance**: Optimization strategies
:::

## Next Steps

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Practice:**

- Implement all three light types
- Create dynamic flashlight
- Add colored lights to scene
- Experiment with attenuation
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Advanced:**

- Cascade shadow maps
- Point light shadows (cube maps)
- Soft shadows (PCF)
- Deferred shading
- PBR lighting
:::
:::

## Resources

**Learning:**

- [Learn OpenGL - Lighting](https://learnopengl.com/Lighting)
- [Learn OpenGL - Advanced Lighting](https://learnopengl.com/Advanced-Lighting)
- Real-Time Rendering (book)
- GPU Gems series

**Tools:**

- RenderDoc - Analyze lighting passes
- Shader debugging tools
- Performance profilers

---

# Questions?

::: {.r-fit-text}
Thank you!
:::

::: {.fragment}
Advanced lighting transforms flat renders into believable, immersive 3D worlds!
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Next**: Framebuffers and post-processing effects! 🎨
:::
